sections.title: Market Pulse: Tech Rebounds Amid Geopolitical Calm

items.headline: World Shares Advance as Tech Stocks Recover; Oil Prices Slip on Ceasefire Hopes
items.summary: Global stock markets were mostly higher on Tuesday, with technology shares leading the rebound after Wall Street experienced a sell-off last week. Oil prices retreated as reports indicated Israel and Iran agreed to a temporary halt in military actions. Investor sentiment was also buoyed by stronger-than-expected May jobs data from the U.S.

items.headline: Intel Surges on AI Chip Order Reports
items.summary: Intel Corporation ($INTC) saw a significant gain, up 11.2%, following reports that Alphabet placed a large order for its AI chips.

items.headline: Micron Rebounds from Friday's Sell-off
items.summary: Micron Technology ($MU) climbed 9.9%, recovering substantially after experiencing its largest S&P 500 loss on Friday.

items.headline: Marvell Technology Added to S&P 500
items.summary: Marvell Technology ($MRVL) advanced 9.6% after S&P Dow Jones Indices announced its inclusion in the widely followed S&P 500 index.

items.headline: Apple Slips After AI Event Disappoints
items.summary: Apple ($AAPL) shares declined 1.9% as its highly anticipated AI overhaul of Siri, unveiled at the annual Worldwide Developers Conference, reportedly underwhelmed investors.

items.headline: Mission Produce Reports Downbeat Q2 Results
items.summary: Mission Produce Inc. ($AVO) fell 5.43% after the company announced weaker-than-expected second-quarter results following Monday's closing bell.

items.headline: U.S. May Jobs Report Exceeds Expectations
items.summary: The May 2026 Nonfarm Payrolls (NFP) report showed the U.S. economy added 172,000 jobs, significantly surpassing the 85,000 forecast. April's figure was also revised upward. Wage growth remained tame at +0.3% monthly and +3.4% annually, indicating an improving labor market without stoking wage inflation.

items.headline: Consumer Inflation Expectations Ease Slightly in May
items.summary: U.S. one-year-ahead inflation expectations in May slipped to 3.5% from 3.6%. Consumers anticipate slower price growth for gas, medical care, and college, but higher prices for homes, food, and rent.

items.headline: Goldman Sachs Adjusts Fed Rate Cut Forecast
items.summary: Goldman Sachs has withdrawn its projection for a December Fed interest rate cut, now expecting the Federal Reserve to maintain unchanged rates until cuts in June 2027 and December 2027.

items.headline: Technology and Energy Lead Year-to-Date Sector Performance
items.summary: Year-to-date, the Energy sector leads all sectors with approximately a 30% return, closely followed by Technology at 28%. Financials, Communication Services, and Consumer Discretionary sectors are currently lagging. The Philadelphia Semiconductor Index saw a notable 5.6% surge today.

items.headline: Oil Prices Retreat as Mideast Tensions Ease
items.summary: Crude oil futures traded lower after Israel and Iran reportedly agreed to halt offensive operations, easing fears of broader conflict in the Middle East. Brent crude fell by 1.75%.

items.headline: Q1 2026 Earnings Season Nears End; Oracle, Adobe Anticipated
items.summary: The first-quarter 2026 earnings season is drawing to a close, with technology being the largest contributor to year-over-year EPS growth. Markets are now awaiting upcoming earnings reports from Oracle on Wednesday and Adobe and Dollarama on Thursday.
